summary: Maintain 4.5:1 contrast for body, 3:1 for bold ≥18px; white on #92278F/#262262/#01A47E passes; avoid silver/periwinkle on white.
All images require alt text; ISI must be live text (never image) to ensure indication and safety with images off.
Accessibility: Live-text min 10px (footnotes), body ≥12px, tap targets ≥44px, logical heading order, exactly one H1 per email.
Use exactly one H1 per email, ensuring all headings follow a logical order for accessibility.
Underline links to ensure accessibility, avoid relying solely on color; use semantic <table role="presentation"> markup for non-data tables.
Each content section is a 600px table with background, top/bottom padding (§2.2), optional wave edge, no outer margins, stacking flush with adjacent sections and Top/End Matter.
Sections declare theme backgrounds at top/bottom; adjacent sections match or wave-transition; first section meets header on white/theme; last section ends with footer-approved wave background.
Footnote symbols use * † ‡ § sequence continuously throughout the email, numbered across sections, resolving above/within locked End Matter per template rules.
Assembly rule: max 1 Wave graphic and 1 key-strategic-question callout per email; max 1 primary CTA per section; approx. 3 CTAs total per email.
No section introduces fonts, colors, button shapes, icons outside this doc; new icons match existing simplified line style/weight; no logo lockup or ISI/legal copy reproduction.
Final assembly QA: verify body size (12 px/1.4), audience matches header/footer (§4.2), all §1.8 content sections included, consistent theme, no horizontal scroll at both breakpoints.
Use brand palette with 30% Purple, 30% Dark Blue, 20% White, 15% Silver/Dark Gray, 5% Green; prioritize Purple, Dark Blue, White as primary colors; others are accents.
Avoid Purple/Green for body text; Green only for CTAs/accents; Dark Gray for charts/secondary UI; Silver not for text.
Explicit text colors: Titles #92278F purple; Headlines & subheads #262262 dark blue; Body copy & ISI #000000 black.
Email title and first line must be purple; no other text in email may use purple.
Highlight on white body sections (excluding headings/sub-headings/titles) must be black and bold; blue or purple disallowed in body paragraphs.
(color_application) Each sentence, heading or body, must use only one color; mixing two colors within the same sentence is prohibited.
In Flip The Script campaign, Periwinkle, Purple, Green used evenly at 33% each as accents, not replacing branded palette colors.
Scene-color pairings fixed: Periwinkle→Office, Purple→Cafe, Green→Soccer; White (Safe White) unpaired and unrestricted.
Callout colors over white: Periwinkle 80%, Purple 60%, Green 40% opacity; White banner over imagery at 80% opacity.
Primary gradient: #262262(0)-#92278F(100) at 45° in section.gradient.primary; light gradient: #CFDCE3 to #FFFFFF in section.gradient.light; rotation allowed.
Dark Blue → Purple gradient applies sparingly to headlines/callouts, e.g., two-tone headline with back half in Purple only.
Gradients in email: only section background or CSS with #92278F/#262262 fallback; no gradient text, use two-color split headline (navy + purple).

Use one theme per email (White, Silver/light, Purple #92278F, Navy #262262, Green #01A47E); frame styles tile, button, campaign-image colors; light themes = dark text + teal buttons; dark themes = white text + knockout logo + light/outlined buttons; no section-level theming within a send.
On Purple/Navy/Green panels use white text and white underlined links; on White/Silver panels navy headlines, black body text, purple accents, and purple/navy underlined links.

DTP emails alternate backgrounds: purple hero → white content (efficacy + side-effects) → white/light photo (story/lifestyle) → green #01A47E accent (affordability/icons) → purple campaign image → locked End Matter.
Sections have no edge margin; transitions use background color changes or full-bleed hero images, not dividers or rules.
Green panel appears max once per email, used exclusively for either affordability block or symptom-icon trio, never both together.
Light backgrounds (White, Silver) use green (#01A47E) filled button with white (#FFFFFF) label; dark backgrounds use white (#FFFFFF) button with green (#01A47E) label.
In CTA, on light backgrounds (White, Silver) heading is #262262, body #000000; on dark backgrounds (Dark Gray, Purple, Dark Blue, Green, Teal, Periwinkle) both are #FFFFFF.

CTA section right image must match background: Email_Primary_2_column.png with purple #92278F (default), Email_Primary_column_golf.png with teal #0F99BC (LPGA content), Cake_lockup.png with periwinkle #7B7BED; use one image and matching background only, swap both together; all backgrounds are dark-group—heading/body/button white, label green.
Use Email_Primary_column_golf.png and Teal #0F99BC background only if email contains LPGA content; Teal #0F99BC is exclusive to LPGA golf CTA section.
If content section is above CTA, CTA shows text+button only on approved background, no right-side image; colors follow the background group rules.
Email layout widths: Desktop 600px, Mobile 375px; both layouts required per component; SFMC auto-serves correct version.
Build emails on 600 px fixed-width table; use fluid/hybrid technique collapsing to 375 px width, fully fluid below 480 px breakpoint.
Component reference heights (Primary 1-Col 600×1138/375×788; Primary 2-Col 600×1088/375×1538; Secondary 600×458) are proportional guidelines, not fixed limits.
All spacing values must be multiples of 8 px; adjust layouts only in 8 px increments, avoiding odd one-off values.
Section side padding 24px desktop/16px mobile, vertical padding 32px desktop/24px mobile, card padding 16px all sides, image-text gap 16px.
Adjacent stacked sections have no margin; spacing is managed by each section’s padding for flush edge-to-edge backgrounds and wave shapes.
Two-column layouts: 24px gutter desktop, 24px vertical gap mobile; 12-column grid over 552px content, half-column 264px + 24px gutter; uses tokens.
Use white-outline rounded rectangle with transparent fill (callout.key_question.border) exclusively for key strategic questions like 'DO YOU WANT TO TRY SOMETHING DIFFERENT?'
Use white banner callout at 80% opacity over background for 'IBSRELA WORKS DIFFERENTLY…' claim.
Scene banners over white: Office = Periwinkle 80% opacity, Cafe = Purple 60%, Soccer = Green 40%; fixed scene/color pairings only.
Use approved icon trio left-to-right: Constipation, Belly Pain, Bloating; each circular green-tinted icon with navy #262262 label beneath (symptom_trio rules).
All three symptom icons must appear together, in order, without isolation or alternate styles.
Symptom-icon trio on green/teal panel or white above it, followed by headline, body copy, and CTA in the layout section.
All text and CTA buttons are left-aligned; CTA buttons align flush with the left edge of the text block above, never centered or right-aligned.
In the CTA section, visuals appear right of text in two-column layout; without visuals, text is left-aligned, full-width.

Parent brand typography: H1 Avenir Black Title Case Dark Blue #262262/Purple #92278F; H2 Avenir Black sentence case Dark Blue/Purple; Body Avenir Roman sentence case Black #000000; ISI subheads Avenir Black ALL CAPS Black; Footnotes Avenir Book sentence case Black; Campaign headline Avenir Black ALL CAPS campaign palette; Callouts Avenir Black ALL CAPS.
Use Avenir Heavy (Arial Bold in email) Black #000000 exclusively for ISI boxed warning text; no other usage allowed.
For email/PowerPoint, substitute Avenir with Arial using 'Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif'; map weights (Black/Heavy→Bold, Roman/Book→Regular), never webfont Avenir in email.
Typography editorial scale: H1 24px desktop/22px mobile Bold 1.25lh; H2 16px Bold 1.5lh; body 16px Regular 1.5lh; CTA 14px Bold 1.2lh single line.
End Matter ISI/legal footer fonts fixed: ISI subheads 14px Bold, RISK INFO 15px Bold, ISI body 13px Regular, footnotes/abbrevs/references/footer 11px Regular, line-height 1.4.
Typography: Body text letter-spacing 0; ALL-CAPS callouts/CTAs +0.5px; no negative tracking in email.
Paragraph spacing in sections: headline→subhead 8px; subhead→body 12px; headline→body (no subhead) 12px; body paragraphs 12px; body→CTA 16px.
Sections own half gaps; Editorial white sections use 12px 24px 12px 24px padding; Dark feature blocks get 24px all sides; Inset cards have 12px radius, 24px padding; side gutters 24px always.
Section padding rules: Editorial→Editorial 24px; Editorial↔Dark feature 36px; Dark feature→Dark feature 48px; full-bleed hero/swirl(0)→edges 12px gap.
Never override vertical padding tokens; never use empty spacer rows or placeholder cells, as they add 12–16px and break spacing calculations.
Headlines use Title Case (h1.case); all other text uses sentence case (body.case, h2.case).
Campaign-specific headlines (FLIP THE SCRIPT) use ALL CAPS, overriding default Title Case (campaign.headline.case).
All caps required for callouts (callout.case) and CTAs (cta.button.label.case) in typography.
Capitalize each word in campaign names within body copy (e.g., Flip The Script), no all caps or lowercase.
In 'FLIP THE SCRIPT' display lockup, 'THE' is upside down; 'ON IBS-C' in Avenir Light at 50% opacity with hyphen replaced by 100% opacity brand swoosh.
Use Arial Bold only for headlines, subheads, card titles, CTA labels, ISI subheads, boxed-warning text, and MLR-approved key claim phrases; avoid using bold for hierarchy.
Avoid bolding entire body paragraphs, footnotes, references, links (underline links only), or over ~10 consecutive words in body copy.
Avoid bold+italic in email body; use Arial Italic only for journal titles in references or as specified in approved copy deck.
SFMC emails stack components in order: (1) locked Top Matter, (2) optional Primary 1 Column, (3) optional Primary 2 Column, (4) repeatable optional Secondary, (5) locked End Matter.
Top Matter and End Matter sections are LOCKED; use supplied components only, do not build or modify.
Select one wireframe template, remove unnecessary sections, do not add components; adjust content to fit templates without altering their structure.
Elements within a section can be removed if unneeded, but no new element types may be added or invented.
Provide Desktop (600px) and Mobile (375px) layouts for each component; SFMC auto-serves based on device; all content sections QA'd at both breakpoints.

First content section aligns flush against locked Top Matter with white/theme background, optional top wave; last content section aligns flush against locked End Matter with wave-edge ISI panel below.
Top Matter locked by audience (DTP: logo without BID, patient-friendly safety; HCP: logo with BID), includes mandatory safety message, links, View in Browser; no IBSRELA logo in content.
Email audience set by header (DTP vs HCP); all content and images must align with the audience from the Top Matter.
End Matter is locked with full FDA-reviewed ISI, Ardelyx contact, copyright/trademark; body copy 12px/1.4 line spacing; coordinate references/footnotes numbering; no ISI duplicates.

Email Primary 1 Column: single column (~600×1138 desktop/375×788 mobile) full-width hero image → purple H1 → bold subheadline → body copy → teal 'Get connected' CTA; theme alters background, optional wave borders top; editable hero image, headlines, body, CTA label.

Email Primary 2 Column layout: two columns (~600×1088 desktop, ~375×1538 mobile, stacked), text left, image right; purple/gradient background, white text, outlined CTA; editable headline, body, CTA, image; options: wave shape, button style.
Email Secondary layout: two-column ~600×458 desktop or stacked ~375×1363 mobile cards with brand image, navy headline, body copy, teal CTA; toggles for columns, image position, subhead, button.
Use second person tone; empathize unresolved symptoms; frame IBSRELA as return to daily life, not clinical performance; lead with empathy before product benefits.
Use "IBS-C symptoms" (constipation, belly pain, and/or bloating), not clinical jargon; always list all three symptoms together when mentioning benefits.
Patient-story modules require disclosure: "Individual results and experiences may vary. [Name] is an actual compensated IBSRELA patient," placed directly below story copy.
Efficacy claims require qualifier "*Results may vary. Study results seen in 12- and 26-week trials…" and limitation "These results observed but analysis can't draw conclusions.
DTP CTA labels: short verb phrases, 1–3 words, sentence case per cta.button.label.case for dtp_patient audience.
Poll/segmentation questions can use a single solid-fill response button styled as a primary CTA, e.g., "Yes, I have.
DTP CTAs must avoid HCP-only label "Get connected" from HCP library.
Rule (cta): Per-section CTAs match section topic—story: "Watch [name]'s story"; product overview: "Explore IBSRELA" or "Learn more about how IBSRELA is different".
